audit_scope: Static and executable audit of both released skill packages, all twelve workspaces, completion validators, deterministic analysis stages, committed Bayesian outputs, and bounded validator mutations. No model calls or scientific experiments were rerun.
paper_release_timing: The pinned commit is after arXiv v1 and before v2; it is official release evidence but not proven byte-identical paper-time or v2-analysis evidence.

configured_system_boundary.paper_reported_agent: Codex with GPT-5.4, Extra High reasoning
configured_system_boundary.paper_reported_local_hardware: MacBook Pro, M4 Max, 128 GB memory
configured_system_boundary.paper_reported_cluster: Purdue Gautschi CPU/GPU resources including H100 nodes
configured_system_boundary.release_observation: Initial/follow-up prompts, target-level computation seeds, commands, timestamps, artifacts, and hashes are retained. Agent provider snapshot/request IDs, agent transcripts, token usage, monetary cost, exact Codex build, OS/package lock, and complete compute-image identity are not retained in the workspaces.
configured_system_boundary.claude_skill_evidence: A Claude Code adaptation is released, but all twelve evaluated runs used Codex; no Claude Code treatment run is included.

deterministic_analysis.outputs: Reproduced 158 target rows, 684 metric rows, 12 effort rows, 388 parsed acceptance predicates, 12 coverage rows, 39 canonical scalar observations, 9 structural rows, and paper-level acceptance-rule agreement. All regenerated files were byte-identical to the archive except canonical_fidelity.csv, whose differences were only last-bit platform float renderings; classifications and headline values were unchanged.
deterministic_analysis.headline_replay: 37/39 scalar observations were inside the hand-curated paper-anchored thresholds; acceptance-rule agreement was PIFT 8/11, PINN-I 4/8, PINN-II 5/11, SINDy 19/20.

bayesian_analysis.rerun_status: blocked in the clean audit environment because jax, numpyro, and matplotlib are undeclared and absent; the release has no Python requirements or lockfile
bayesian_analysis.committed_artifact_status: Committed summaries, draws, sampler extras, posterior-predictive arrays, diagnostics tables, and plots are present. The committed diagnostics report max R-hat 1.00483, minimum effective sample size 1387.62, and zero divergences. These are inspected outputs, not an independent rerun.

mutation_probes.implication: Completion is closed-world relative to the agent-authored target inventory; it does not establish source-claim completeness.

mutation_probes.mutation: Changed recorded comparison values for fig_ex1_posterior_collapse to violate std_monotone==1 and std_ratio_beta1e5_to_1e2<=0.25.
mutation_probes.implication: The completion validator checks that comparison metrics exist but does not execute the target tolerance predicates.

mutation_probes.mutation: Replaced report/main.tex with comments containing only each output_path and report_anchor, while retaining the old PDF.
mutation_probes.implication: Report coverage is substring presence, not evidence that claims, results, caveats, or artifacts are substantively reported or that the PDF corresponds to current TeX.

mutation_probes.mutation: Changed the linked run-0025 expected-artifact SHA-256 to all zeros in run_index.jsonl.
mutation_probes.implication: The validator checks current artifact-to-provenance hash and expected path but does not bind that artifact hash to the linked execution ledger's expected-artifact hash.

mutation_probes.mutation: Replaced a paper trace with the string 'asserted paper trace' and updated its provenance hash.
mutation_probes.implication: Trace existence and self-consistent hashing do not establish source entailment, method fidelity, or locator adequacy.

mutation_probes.mutation: Removed the provenance record for fig_ex1_posterior_collapse.
mutation_probes.validator_result: failed with an explicit missing-provenance error
mutation_probes.implication: The validator genuinely enforces record presence while leaving important record semantics and cross-record bindings unchecked.

release_findings: The deterministic extraction, coverage, structural, and rule-drift stages are substantially replayable from the archive.
The two skill packages express the same workflow but differ in interface paths/tool declarations; only Codex was evaluated.
Five distinct paper_harness.py hashes occur across the twelve workspaces, so validator implementation is not one immutable treatment across all runs.

Each workspace has paper-specific prompts and copied source materials; the release preserves source materials but only the three PIFT copies include an explicit License.txt. Reuse rights for every embedded source-paper artifact should not be inferred from the repository's Apache-2.0 license.

The semantic target alignment was produced by four Claude Opus agents; the planned verify-stage alias was unavailable, and headline mappings were hand-confirmed by the authors. This is an analysis-stage human/model intervention, not a purely deterministic cross-run mapping.

The scalar fidelity table is hand-curated and uses author-selected paper anchors, unit corrections, and thresholds. It productively exposes two completion/fidelity disagreements but is not independent scientific adjudication.
No no-skill arm, cross-model arm, cross-harness arm, independent scientific reviewers, independent target inventory, repeated judging, or exact package-cost ledger is released.

supported: The released workspaces satisfy the released record-presence and self-consistency completion checks for their recorded target sets.
The workflow preserves inspectable target, execution, artifact, provenance, comparison, status, and report-locator records.
Within four selected scientific-ML papers and one configured Codex system, repeated runs show target-decomposition, scalar-output, elapsed-time, correction-path, and acceptance-rule variation.
A separate hand-curated analysis identifies 37/39 scalar observations inside its declared paper-anchored thresholds and two completion/fidelity disagreements.
unsupported: validator soundness for acceptance predicates or source entailment
